TITLE.value: Higher Education Student Records Enterprise Reference Model

Scope.value: UK Higher Education — Student Records and associated enterprise systems

Disclaimer.value: This model is a reference framework for higher education institutions. It represents a generalised view of a Student Records ecosystem and should be adapted to reflect the specific systems, integration patterns, governance arrangements, and regulatory obligations of the implementing institution. RevelationCore accepts no liability for decisions made on the basis of this model without appropriate institutional validation.

systems.Description: Core student records and academic administration platform managing student identity, enrolment, module registration, academic record, assessment result aggregation, progression, and awards. Acts as the authoritative system of record for all ratified academic outcomes, for the operational effects of approved reasonable adjustments, and for the recording of approved exceptional circumstances outcomes for board consideration. Responsible for generating and supplying the data that underpins board papers and candidate profiles for Exam Board consideration, recording and locking ratified outcomes, and distributing those outcomes to downstream operational systems.
systems.Key Capabilities: Enrolment & registration; Academic records & result aggregation; Assessment processing and validation; Moderation workflow support; Grades & progression; Graduation & award management; Board data and candidate profile generation; Approved adjustment outcome management; Approved exceptional circumstances outcome recording; Post-ratification record locking; Downstream outcome distribution

systems.Description: Define and maintain programmes, modules, and learning outcomes.
systems.Key Capabilities: Programme/module design; Curriculum change tracking; Learning outcome mapping; Approval workflows

systems.Description: Create and manage academic timetables for classes, rooms, and other resources.
systems.Key Capabilities: Schedule generation; Room allocation; Conflict resolution; Calendar integration

systems.Description: Manage interactions and relationships with prospects, students, alumni, and partners.
systems.Key Capabilities: Lead management; Communication tracking; Recruitment & marketing automation; Stakeholder engagement

systems.Description: Manage library resources, loans, and digital/electronic materials.
systems.Key Capabilities: Cataloguing; Circulation/loans; E-resource management; Discovery & search services

systems.Description: Handle financial transactions, budgeting, accounting, and procurement.
systems.Key Capabilities: Accounts payable/receivable; Budgeting & forecasting; Procurement; Financial reporting

systems.Description: Provide unified, role-based web access to institutional services and information.
systems.Key Capabilities: Single sign-on; Personalised dashboards; Self-service functionality; Communication tools

systems.Description: Track student attendance and engagement indicators and analyse learning activities.
systems.Key Capabilities: Attendance tracking; Engagement analytics; Early-warning alerts; Reporting/dashboards

systems.Description: Deliver and manage online learning content, assessments, and collaboration tools.
systems.Key Capabilities: Course content hosting; Online assessments; Discussion forums; Gradebook

systems.Description: Manage on-campus accommodation and external event bookings.
systems.Key Capabilities: Room allocation; Conference/venue scheduling; Booking/payment processing; Resource allocation

systems.Description: Manage property portfolios, maintenance, space allocation, and campus infrastructure.
systems.Key Capabilities: Facilities management; Room allocation; Maintenance scheduling; Capital project management

systems.Description: Provision and manage user identities and access rights across enterprise systems.
systems.Key Capabilities: User provisioning; Authentication; Role-based access; Single sign-on

systems.Description: Store, manage, and track electronic documents and records throughout their lifecycle.
systems.Key Capabilities: Document version control; Record retention policies; Secure access; Compliance & auditing

systems.Description: Electronic identity verification service for student personal data and documents.
systems.Key Capabilities: Document scanning; ID matching; Fraud detection; Verification status updates

systems.Description: Provide data analysis, dashboards, and reporting across institution-wide data sets.
systems.Key Capabilities: Data aggregation; Analytics & dashboards; Predictive insights; Performance reporting

systems.Description: Consolidate institutional data from multiple sources for analysis and reporting.
systems.Key Capabilities: Data extraction/ETL; Historical data storage; Centralised reporting; Integration with BI tools

systems.Description: Collect and analyse student feedback on modules, lecturers, and teaching effectiveness.
systems.Key Capabilities: Feedback surveys; Data analysis & reporting; Action tracking; Quality enhancement

systems.Description: Support human resource processes including recruitment, performance, and payroll setup.
systems.Key Capabilities: Staff recruitment & onboarding; Performance reviews; Training & development; Leave management

systems.Description: Execute payroll runs, deductions, tax, and pension management for institutional employees and student bursaries.
systems.Key Capabilities: Payroll calculation; Tax & pension contributions; Bursary & stipend processing; Compliance reporting

systems.Description: Centralise and track research outputs, publications, and researcher profiles.
systems.Key Capabilities: Publications management; Researcher profiles; Compliance reporting; Collaboration tracking

systems.Description: Support the submission, management, and tracking of research funding and contracts.
systems.Key Capabilities: Grant application workflow; Compliance checks; Award management; Contract tracking

systems.Description: Manage and publish digital content for websites, intranets, and portals.
systems.Key Capabilities: Web content creation; Content workflow; Version control; Publishing & archiving

systems.Description: Coordinate and track IT support, incidents, requests, and assets.
systems.Key Capabilities: Incident management; Service request tracking; Configuration & asset management; Knowledge base

systems.Description: Facilitate system-to-system integration and message brokering between applications.
systems.Key Capabilities: Message routing; Data transformation; Enterprise integration patterns; Event-driven architecture

systems.Description: Manage student wellbeing, disability declarations, mental health support, reasonable adjustments, and exceptional circumstances casework.
systems.Key Capabilities: Disability assessment & DSA; Reasonable adjustments management; Exceptional circumstances case management; Mental health case management; Early intervention alerts

systems.Description: Plan and manage the operational logistics of formal examinations, including timetabling, room allocation, invigilation scheduling, seating plans, and candidate entry management. Exam Scheduling is responsible for the physical and logistical delivery of examinations. Academic governance — including moderation, classification, external examiner scrutiny, and formal ratification — sits with the Exam Board, supported by Student Records and any local assessment administration tooling.
systems.Key Capabilities: Exam timetabling; Room and invigilation allocation; Seating plan generation; Candidate entry management; Accommodation logistics (separate rooms, rest break scheduling)

systems.Description: Manage academic misconduct allegations, investigation workflows, panel decisions, and penalties arising from plagiarism, collusion, cheating, or other breaches of academic integrity regulations.
systems.Key Capabilities: Misconduct case management; Evidence handling; Investigation workflow; Panel decision recording; Penalty determination; Outcome notification

systems.Description: Formal academic decision-making body responsible for reviewing, confirming, and ratifying student results in accordance with institutional regulations and sector expectations. Operates at two levels: the Module Assessment Board, which confirms and moderates individual module marks and ensures marking consistency; and the Award and Progression Board, which determines degree classifications, progression decisions, resit requirements, compensation and condonement outcomes, and final awards. The Exam Board reviews candidate profiles and board data generated from Student Records, considers exceptional circumstances under institutional regulations, and formally ratifies outcomes. External examiner confirmation forms part of the ratification process before results are locked. Ratified decisions are authoritative and cannot be amended without formal institutional process such as appeal or approved correction under regulation.
systems.Key Capabilities: Module result confirmation and moderation oversight; Degree classification and award decisions; Progression and resit determinations; Exceptional circumstances consideration; Compensation and condonement application; Outcome sign-off and record lock authorisation

systems.Description: Independent academic appointed from another UK higher education institution to assure the comparability of academic standards and the fairness of marking across cohorts. Reviews a sample of assessed work and supporting data made available through institutional systems, scrutinises pre-board calculations and classification decisions, and attends the Exam Board to challenge or endorse outcomes. External examiner confirmation forms part of the ratification process before results are formally confirmed and published. Submits an annual report to the institution in line with sector expectations.
systems.Key Capabilities: Sample marking and moderation review; Standards comparability assurance; Pre-board classification scrutiny; Exam Board attendance and challenge; Annual report submission; Ratification confirmation

systems.Description: Universities and Colleges Admissions Service — the national admissions processing body for UK higher education.
systems.Key Capabilities: Application processing; Offer and acceptance management; Clearing coordination; Qualification verification

systems.Description: Higher Education Statistics Agency — statutory body responsible for collecting and publishing data about UK higher education.
systems.Key Capabilities: Statutory student data collection; HESA ID assignment; Data quality validation; Sector benchmarking

systems.Description: Government-owned company administering student loans and grants on behalf of the UK government.
systems.Key Capabilities: Tuition fee loan processing; Maintenance loan administration; Enrolment confirmation; Overpayment recovery

systems.Description: Home Office agency responsible for student visa compliance; institutions hold a Student sponsor licence.
systems.Key Capabilities: CAS management; Attendance monitoring compliance; Visa status tracking; Sponsor compliance reporting

interactions.TEXT: Programme and module catalogue updates
interactions.Description: Curriculum Management is the official source of programmes, modules, and prerequisites. This flow updates the Student Records catalogue with current academic structures, ensuring consistent enrolment, registration, and progression records.

interactions.TEXT: Enrolment, completion, and performance metrics
interactions.Description: Student Records provides enrolment, completion, and performance metrics to Curriculum Management. These analytics inform data-driven updates to programmes, modules, and prerequisites.

interactions.TEXT: Student enrolment and module registration data
interactions.Description: Student Records provides key enrolment information needed by the Timetabling system to create accurate class schedules.

interactions.TEXT: Finalised timetable and room allocation data
interactions.Description: The Timetabling system sends finalised scheduling information and related updates back to Student Records so students and administrative staff have consistent data.

interactions.TEXT: Applicant, offer, and admissions decision data
interactions.Description: The CRM system manages prospective student enquiries, applications, and admissions decisions before individuals become enrolled students. This data flows to Student Records so that the institution can officially register admitted individuals.

interactions.TEXT: Student enrolment status and progression updates
interactions.Description: Once a prospect becomes an enrolled student, Student Records manages their academic progress. Relevant updates flow back to the CRM so the institution can maintain accurate engagement information and communications.

interactions.TEXT: Library loans, fines, and overdue notifications
interactions.Description: The Library system sends circulation updates and overdue notifications to Student Records so that the student record accurately reflects any outstanding obligations or statuses.

interactions.TEXT: Student enrolment status and access rights
interactions.Description: Student Records provides the Library with up-to-date student status and registration data so library privileges can be granted or restricted appropriately.

interactions.TEXT: Enrolment and tuition fee liability data
interactions.Description: Student Records provides Finance with key enrolment and fee-related data so the institution can accurately bill students and set up payment plans.

interactions.TEXT: Payment confirmations and financial hold status
interactions.Description: Finance is the system of record for financial transactions, invoices, and payment processing. Once a financial event occurs, Finance transmits the outcome to Student Records, which becomes the system of record for the effect on the student's standing.

interactions.TEXT: Student self-service updates and module selections
interactions.Description: The Enterprise Web Portal serves as a central access point for students to submit or update personal information, application details, and module selections.

interactions.TEXT: Enrolment status, timetables, grades, and notifications
interactions.Description: Student Records holds the authoritative information on each student's enrolment, schedule, and academic progress, which is surfaced in the Enterprise Web Portal.

interactions.TEXT: Attendance records and absence alerts
interactions.Description: Attendance Monitoring captures data about student presence in lectures, seminars, labs, or other learning activities. This data is forwarded to Student Records to keep a consolidated record of student engagement.

interactions.TEXT: Student roster and academic calendar data
interactions.Description: Student Records is the main repository of enrolment and course information. This data flows to Attendance Monitoring so the system knows which students are associated with each class or activity.

interactions.TEXT: Student enrolment, course assignments, and term dates
interactions.Description: Student Records is the authoritative source of enrolment and course information. This data is pushed to the VLE so that the correct students and instructors have access to the right course materials at the right times.

interactions.TEXT: Assessment grades, completion status, and academic alerts
interactions.Description: Once assessments are completed within the VLE, key results flow back into Student Records to support the official academic record and downstream assessment processing.

interactions.TEXT: Student profiles, enrolment status, and programme details
interactions.Description: Student Records is the authoritative source of official student information. It sends necessary details to the Accommodation system to facilitate housing allocations and ensure eligibility.

interactions.TEXT: Room allocation, booking status, and check-in/out records
interactions.Description: The Accommodation system tracks booking and occupancy details. These updates flow back into Student Records for record-keeping, financial planning, and student support tracking.

interactions.TEXT: Enrolment volumes, timetable data, and occupancy forecasts
interactions.Description: Student Records holds the authoritative enrolment information and academic timetables. The Estates system relies on this data to plan and manage campus facilities effectively.

interactions.TEXT: Room allocation confirmations and availability updates
interactions.Description: Estates captures building usage, room allocations, and event schedules. This information is sent back to Student Records for accurate record-keeping and to inform academic scheduling.

interactions.TEXT: Student identity, enrolment status, and access eligibility
interactions.Description: Student Records is the authoritative source of student data. This system sends key information to IAM so users can be provisioned with appropriate access rights.

interactions.TEXT: Credential updates, account locks, and role assignments
interactions.Description: IAM manages the authentication and authorisation layers for institutional applications. This data flows back to Student Records to keep records consistent with login activity and security status.

interactions.TEXT: Enrolment forms, transcripts, and graduation certificates
interactions.Description: Student Records is the main source of official student data. Relevant documents are transmitted to the EDRMS for secure storage, version control, and compliance with archival policies.

interactions.TEXT: Archived record confirmations, document links, and access logs
interactions.Description: After documents are archived or updated in the EDRMS, it sends relevant status information or version links back to Student Records.

interactions.TEXT: Student identity data and verification request
interactions.Description: Student Records initiates ID verification checks by sending essential student data and relevant documentation to the Online ID Verification service.

interactions.TEXT: Verification outcome, confidence scores, and fraud flags
interactions.Description: Once the verification process is completed, the Online ID Verification service sends the outcome, along with supporting details, back to Student Records.

interactions.TEXT: Student performance, enrolment, and progression data
interactions.Description: Student Records provides the Business Intelligence platform with structured extracts of student performance, enrolment volumes, module outcomes, and retention data.

interactions.TEXT: At-risk flags and predictive intervention alerts
interactions.Description: Business Intelligence is the system of record for the analytical process. Once a flag is written to Student Records, SIS becomes the system of record for the intervention record.

interactions.TEXT: Bulk student data extracts for longitudinal warehousing
interactions.Description: Student Records provides the Data Warehouse with full and incremental extracts of student data, including enrolment history, academic outcomes, module registrations, and demographic records.

interactions.TEXT: Data quality reports and reconciliation alerts
interactions.Description: The Data Warehouse performs cross-system reconciliation and data quality checks. Discrepancy reports and data quality alerts are returned to Student Records to support record accuracy and audit readiness.

interactions.TEXT: Module roster and student registration data for survey distribution
interactions.Description: Student Records provides the Student Evaluation of Teaching system with the authoritative list of students registered on each module, enabling targeted and timely survey distribution.

interactions.TEXT: Survey completion rates and aggregated feedback scores
interactions.Description: Once surveys close, the Student Evaluation system returns completion statistics and aggregated module-level feedback scores to Student Records.

interactions.TEXT: Graduate Teaching Assistant records and student-staff roles
interactions.Description: Student Records provides HR with the details of students holding dual roles, such as Graduate Teaching Assistants, research assistants, or student ambassadors.

interactions.TEXT: Academic supervisor and personal tutor assignments
interactions.Description: HR is the system of record for the staff assignment process. Once an assignment is confirmed, the outcome flows to Student Records, which becomes the system of record for the academic relationship as it affects the student.

interactions.TEXT: Bursary-eligible student records and GTA payment authorisations
interactions.Description: Student Records provides Payroll with authorised lists of students eligible for bursaries, hardship funds, or stipend payments, along with GTA hours approved for payment.

interactions.TEXT: Bursary and stipend payment confirmations
interactions.Description: Payroll confirms to Student Records when bursary, stipend, or hardship payments have been processed.

interactions.TEXT: Research student enrolment and researcher profile data
interactions.Description: Student Records provides the Current Research Information System with enrolment data for postgraduate research students.

interactions.TEXT: Research degree milestones and publication records
interactions.Description: CRIS returns research progression milestones — such as confirmation of registration, thesis submission, and viva outcomes — along with publication records to Student Records.

interactions.TEXT: Student researcher eligibility and supervisor assignment data
interactions.Description: Student Records provides Research Proposals with verified details of students named on grant applications, their enrolment status, programme of study, and assigned supervisors.

interactions.TEXT: Funded research studentship award records
interactions.Description: Research Proposals is the system of record for the funding process. Once a studentship is confirmed, the award outcome flows to Student Records, which becomes the system of record for what that funding means for the student academically and financially.

interactions.TEXT: Student cohort and programme data for content personalisation
interactions.Description: Student Records provides the Content Management System with cohort, programme, and faculty data to enable targeted and personalised content delivery.

interactions.TEXT: Published regulatory and policy notifications
interactions.Description: The CMS notifies Student Records when significant regulatory, policy, or procedural documents are published that require annotation against student records.

interactions.TEXT: Student identity and account data for incident context
interactions.Description: Student Records provides the IT Service Management platform with student identity, enrolment status, and system access details.

interactions.TEXT: Service request outcomes affecting student account status
interactions.Description: ITSM notifies Student Records of resolved service requests or incidents that result in changes to a student's account status.

interactions.TEXT: Application data, offer decisions, and clearing records
interactions.Description: UCAS transmits applicant data, including personal details, qualification records, programme preferences, and offer/acceptance decisions to Student Records.

interactions.TEXT: Enrolment confirmation and withdrawal notifications
interactions.Description: Student Records confirms successful enrolment back to UCAS once an applicant has registered, and notifies UCAS of withdrawals, deferrals, or no-shows.

interactions.TEXT: Statutory student data return
interactions.Description: Student Records submits the annual statutory HESA Student record return, covering enrolled students, programme details, module registrations, qualifications awarded, and demographic data.

interactions.TEXT: Validation reports and HESA student identifiers
interactions.Description: HESA is the authority for assigning student identifiers and validating statutory submission data. Once identifiers are assigned and validation outcomes are returned, Student Records becomes the system of record for storing and propagating the HESA student identifier.

interactions.TEXT: Enrolment confirmation for tuition fee and maintenance loan release
interactions.Description: Student Records confirms to the Student Loans Company that an eligible student is enrolled and attending, triggering the release of tuition fee loans to the institution and maintenance loan payments to the student.

interactions.TEXT: Loan entitlement, payment status, and overpayment notifications
interactions.Description: The Student Loans Company is the system of record for the loan process. Once a loan decision or payment event occurs, Student Records becomes the system of record for the fee liability and financial standing implications for the student.

interactions.TEXT: CAS creation requests and attendance compliance data
interactions.Description: Student Records provides UKVI with Confirmation of Acceptance for Studies creation requests for international students requiring a Student visa, along with ongoing attendance monitoring data required under the institution's sponsor licence obligations.

interactions.TEXT: Visa status updates and sponsor compliance alerts
interactions.Description: UKVI notifies the institution of changes to a student's visa status, including grants, refusals, curtailments, and compliance alerts.

interactions.TEXT: Student profiles, disability declarations, and academic records
interactions.Description: Student Records provides the Student Wellbeing and Disability system with core student data, including declared disabilities, programme details, academic performance indicators, and any existing flags or holds.

interactions.TEXT: Learning analytics and digital engagement data
interactions.Description: The VLE provides the Business Intelligence platform with detailed learning analytics, including login frequency, content access patterns, assessment submission behaviours, and discussion forum engagement.

interactions.TEXT: Attendance trends and engagement analytics
interactions.Description: Attendance Monitoring provides the BI platform with aggregated and individual attendance trend data, absence patterns, and engagement scores.

interactions.TEXT: Consolidated multi-system data feeds for analytics
interactions.Description: The Data Warehouse provides the BI platform with consolidated, cleansed, and historicised data drawn from multiple source systems including Student Records, Finance, HR, and the VLE.

interactions.TEXT: Personalised communications and targeted content
interactions.Description: The CRM system pushes personalised communications, targeted notifications, and campaign content to the Enterprise Web Portal based on student segment, lifecycle stage, or engagement profile.

interactions.TEXT: Approved adjustment outcomes for course content and assessment delivery
interactions.Description: Student Records distributes approved reasonable adjustment outcomes to the VLE. The VLE must consume adjustment data from Student Records, not directly from the Wellbeing system, to ensure a single consistent and auditable record. Adjustments distributed include extended submission deadlines, alternative assessment formats, accessible content requirements, and assessment accommodations.

interactions.TEXT: Approved attendance adjustment outcomes
interactions.Description: Student Records distributes approved attendance adjustment outcomes to Attendance Monitoring. Attendance Monitoring must consume adjustment data from Student Records, not directly from the Wellbeing system.

interactions.TEXT: Student exam entries, module registrations, and approved examination accommodations
interactions.Description: Student Records provides the Exam Scheduling system with the data required for operational examination delivery: the authoritative list of students entered for each assessment, their programme and level details, and approved physical examination accommodations held in Student Records. Exceptional circumstances flags and academic governance data are surfaced to the Exam Board via Student Records board preparation, not via Exam Scheduling.

interactions.TEXT: Finalised exam timetables, seating plans, and candidate numbers
interactions.Description: The Exam Scheduling system returns finalised examination timetables, candidate seating assignments, and candidate numbers to Student Records for publication via the student portal. Academic outcomes are returned to Student Records by the Exam Board following ratification, not by Exam Scheduling.

interactions.TEXT: Approved reasonable adjustment outcomes
interactions.Description: The Student Wellbeing and Disability system is the system of record for the reasonable adjustment process — owning the case, the evidence, the assessment, and the approval decision. Reasonable adjustments cover ongoing accommodations for disability or long-term health conditions, including DSA entitlements, examination accommodations, and, where applicable, system-enforced operational rule treatments such as late penalty suppression or extended submission windows. Once a determination is reached, the approved outcome is transmitted to Student Records. Student Records becomes the system of record for the academic operational effect of that decision and is solely responsible for distributing adjustment outcomes to downstream systems. No downstream system should receive adjustment data directly from the Wellbeing system.

interactions.TEXT: Board data, candidate result profiles, exceptional circumstances outcomes, approved adjustment indicators, misconduct outcomes, and calculated recommendations
interactions.Description: Student Records prepares and presents the complete dataset required for formal Exam Board consideration. This includes module marks aggregated from all assessment sources, pre-board calculated profiles and degree classification recommendations generated under institutional rules, approved exceptional circumstances outcomes, approved reasonable adjustment indicators where relevant to board visibility, and academic misconduct outcomes. Student Records is the system of record for all of this data. The Exam Board reviews this material, applies academic judgement, and formally ratifies outcomes.

interactions.TEXT: Ratified academic decisions and board outcomes
interactions.Description: The Exam Board returns formally approved academic decisions to Student Records, which becomes the authoritative and locked record of all ratified outcomes. This includes confirmed module marks, progression decisions, degree classifications, resit requirements, compensation and condonement decisions, and the outcomes of exceptional circumstances considered under institutional regulations. External examiner confirmation forms part of the ratification process before outcomes are confirmed and recorded. Once ratified outcomes are written to Student Records and the record is locked, amendments can only be made through formal institutional process.

interactions.TEXT: Approved exceptional circumstances outcomes
interactions.Description: The Student Wellbeing and Disability system is the system of record for the exceptional circumstances process — managing student submissions of personal, medical, or compassionate circumstances, reviewing evidence, and determining whether a claim is upheld. Exceptional circumstances are distinct from reasonable adjustments: they are ad hoc, time-bound requests for a specific event to be taken into account at the Exam Board rather than ongoing operational accommodations. Once a determination is reached, the approved outcome is transmitted to Student Records. Student Records becomes the system of record for the flag against the student and relevant module, and is responsible for surfacing it to the Exam Board as part of board preparation.

interactions.TEXT: Assessment sample access, candidate profiles, and pre-board calculations for external review
interactions.Description: Student Records provides or coordinates access for the External Examiner to the data required to carry out their standards assurance role prior to and during the Exam Board. This includes sampled assessed work made available through institutional assessment systems, full candidate result profiles, pre-board classification calculations, and flagged borderline or exceptional cases. All data is sourced or coordinated through the same authoritative institutional record used for board consideration.

interactions.TEXT: Standards assurance confirmation and ratification input
interactions.Description: The External Examiner provides the Exam Board with independent standards assurance commentary, including scrutiny of marking standards, commentary on borderline or contentious cases, and confirmation that outcomes are fair and comparable with sector expectations. This confirmation forms part of the ratification process before outcomes are finally confirmed and recorded.

interactions.TEXT: Academic misconduct outcomes and penalties
interactions.Description: The Academic Integrity system is the system of record for academic misconduct investigations and decisions. Once a case is concluded, the confirmed outcome and any associated penalty are transmitted to Student Records. Student Records becomes the system of record for the student-facing academic consequence of the decision and surfaces relevant misconduct outcomes to the Exam Board where required under institutional regulations.

interactions.TEXT: Student identity, module registrations, and submission context for misconduct case management
interactions.Description: Student Records provides the Academic Integrity system with the student identity data, module and assessment registrations, and submission records required to initiate and manage misconduct cases. Academic Integrity is the system of record for the investigation and decision process; Student Records is the system of record for the outcome. This flow ensures that case management in Academic Integrity is grounded in the authoritative institutional record of who submitted what and when.
